case_title: S.F. TOWN TAXI INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY ET AL

docket_entries.description: MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCOS DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 61242515) FILED BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HAYASHI, CHRISTIANE IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS DIRECTOR OF TAXI SERVICES

SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100022684), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF S.F. TOWN TAXI INC. SERVED SEP-14-2017, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON CORPORATION AS TO DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED (TRANSACTION ID # 61343315) FILED BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND CHRISTIANE HAYASHI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SF TOWN TAXI INC. - SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CLAIMS. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. "[A] LITIGANT SEEKING TO PLEAD THE BREACH OF A MANDATORY DUTY MUST SPECIFICALLY ALLEGE THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OR REGULATION." (LEHTO V. CITY OF OXNARD (1985) 171 CAL.APP.3D 285, 292.) TOWN TAXI FAILS TO IDENTIFY AN ACTIONABLE MANDATORY DUTY. THE PREAMBLE TO ARTICLE VIIIA IS GENERAL AND ASPIRATIONAL. IT DOES NOT IMPOSE SPECIFIED OFFICIAL ACTION AND CANNOT BE A BASIS FOR LIABILITY. "AN ENACTMENT CREATES A MANDATORY DUTY IF IT REQUIRES A PUBLIC AGENCY TO TAKE A PARTICULAR ACTION. AN ENACTMENT DOES NOT CREATE A MANDATORY DUTY IF IT MERELY RECITES LEGISLATIVE GOALS AND POLICIES THAT MUST BE IMPLEMENTED THROUGH A PUBLIC AGENCY'S EXERCISE OF DISCRETION." (SAN MATEO UNION HIGH SCHOOL DIST. V. COUNTY OF SAN MATEO (2013) 213 CAL.APP.4TH 418, 429 (INTERNAL CITATION OMITTED).) TOWN TAXI ALSO FAILS TO ALLEGE EITHER COMPLIANCE WITH OR AN EXCUSE FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TOWN TAXI IS GRANTED LEAVE TO CORRECT THESE DEFICIENCIES. FOR GUIDANCE OF THE PARTIES GOING FORWARD, THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANTS' GOVERNMENT CODE 820.2 ARGUMENT BECAUSE THE SELECTION OF A PAYMENT TERMINAL IS NOT A BASIC POLICY DECISION COVERED BY THE DISCRETIONARY ACT IMMUNITY. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jun-12-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Continued To Apr-04-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney, Diana Rosenstein of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3933) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and CHRISTIANE HAYASHI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to both causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff SF Town Taxi Inc. is SUSTAINED with twenty days leave to amend as to both claims. The complaint fails to state a cause of action. "[A] litigant seeking to plead the breach of a mandatory duty must specifically allege the applicable statute or regulation." (Lehto v. City of Oxnard (1985) 171 Cal.App.3d 285, 292.) Town Taxi fails to identify an actionable mandatory duty. The Preamble to Article VIIIA is general and aspirational. It does not impose specified official action and cannot be a basis for liability. "An enactment creates a mandatory duty if it requires a public agency to take a particular action. An enactment does not create a mandatory duty if it merely recites legislative goals and policies that must be implemented through a public agency's exercise of discretion." (San Mateo Union High School Dist. v. County of San Mateo (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 418, 429 (internal citation omitted).) Town Taxi also fails to allege either compliance with or an excuse for noncompliance with the Government Claims Act. If it can do so in good faith, Town Taxi is granted leave to correct these deficiencies. For guidance of the parties going forward, the court rejects defendants' Government Code 820.2 argument because the selection of a payment terminal is not a basic policy decision covered by the discretionary act immunity. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)
